The Khmer Rouge was led by Pol Pot, a Cambodian politician and leader of the communist party. He believed that the only way to achieve this was to eliminate the educated class, intellectuals, and anyone who was perceived as a threat to his regime.

Under Pol Pot's leadership, the Khmer Rouge implemented policies that resulted in the deaths of an estimated 1.7 to 2.2 million people, out of a population of approximately 8 million, through executions, forced labor, starvation, and disease. This period is known as the Cambodian Genocide.

Some of the Khmer Rouge's policies included:

* Forcing people to work in collective farms, often under harsh conditions

* Eliminating the educated class, including teachers, doctors, and engineers
* Imposing a strict agrarian lifestyle, where people were forced to grow their own food and live in simple conditions
* Executing anyone who was perceived as a threat to the regime, including intellectuals, artists, and members of the middle class

The Khmer Rouge's rule ended in 1979, when the Vietnamese army invaded Cambodia and overthrew the regime. Pol Pot and many of his top leaders fled to the countryside, where they continued to wage a guerrilla war against the new government. Pol Pot died in 1998, while under house arrest.

It's worth noting that the Khmer Rouge's policies and actions were widely condemned by the international community, and their regime is considered one of the most brutal and genocidal in modern history.
